ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] (American Government) Participate in the [Constitution Day Poster Design Contest](https://www.constitutionday.cc/) (Deadline: October)
[ ] (American Government) Participate in the [National Constitution Bee.](https://nationalconstitutionbee.org/)
[ ] (American Government) Participate in the [Civics Challenge](https://oconnorinstitute.org/cc/). (Information available in the Fall.)
[ ] (Plutarch) Explore the Plutarch Podcast and other resources available on [this website](https://grammaticus.co/resources-for-reading-plutarch/). (Teachers should preview all content.)
[ ] (American Government) Take a[ tour ](https://youtu.be/gw-nUXC-Zjs?si=_u5XeWVtxMIgd0aV)of the National Archives in Washington, D.C., where the public can view the Constitution.
[ ] (Plutarch) Explore the Plutarch Podcast and other resources available on [this website](https://grammaticus.co/resources-for-reading-plutarch/). (Teachers should preview all content.)

[ ] (Plutarch) If Plutarch were alive today, what political or military leaders do you think he would write about? Write a modern version of Plutarch's Lives featuring one (or comparing two) of these modern leaders. 
[ ] (Plutarch) Explore the Plutarch Podcast and other resources available on [this website](https://grammaticus.co/resources-for-reading-plutarch/). (Teachers should preview all content.)
